blocks.body: Past Perfect: Forms and Use — had + V3 for an action that happened before another past action or before a past moment.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In Past Perfect, we use had + V3 when we want to show an action that happened earlier than another past action or before a past moment.","rules":[{"label":"had + V3","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic affirmative model for all subjects.","example":"I had finished. / They had left."},{"label":"Earlier past","color":"blue","text":"The tense shows which event came first in the past.","example":"She had gone home before I called."},{"label":"Ready before then","color":"amber","text":"Use it when a result was already complete by a past moment.","example":"By noon, we had completed the task."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember the Past Perfect form for each group of subjects.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"had + V3","subtitle":"I had finished. / They had arrived."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"had + V3","subtitle":"She had left. / It had stopped."},{"label":"Regular and irregular","title":"worked / finished / gone / seen","subtitle":"After had, use V3, not V1 or V2."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"earlier action / ready result","subtitle":"One action happened earlier or was already complete by another past moment."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Past Perfect","sections":[{"label":"Earlier action","color":"emerald","description":"Show that this action happened before another event in the past.","examples":[{"en":"They had eaten before the lesson started.","ua":"The action happened earlier."},{"en":"I had packed my bag before the taxi arrived.","ua":"Packing was complete first."}]},{"label":"Before a past time","color":"blue","description":"Talk about a result that already existed then.","examples":[{"en":"By 5 p.m., she had written three emails.","ua":"The result was ready by that time."},{"en":"We had found the address by that time.","ua":"The action was already complete then."}]},{"label":"Reason or explanation","color":"amber","description":"Past Perfect often explains why something happened later.","examples":[{"en":"He was upset because he had lost his wallet.","ua":"The earlier action explains the later feeling."},{"en":"She was tired because she had slept badly.","ua":"The earlier situation explains the result."}]},{"label":"Stories and narratives","color":"violet","description":"The tense helps make the order of events clear in a story.","examples":[{"en":"After they had closed the shop, they went home.","ua":"First they closed the shop, then they went home."},{"en":"We had seen the place before we moved there.","ua":"Seeing the place happened earlier."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form check","intro":"See how had works with V3.","rows":[{"en":"I had finished my homework before dinner.","ua":"had + finished","note":"I + had + V3"},{"en":"She had gone home before I called.","ua":"had + gone","note":"she + had + V3"},{"en":"We had seen that film before.","ua":"had + seen","note":"we + had + V3"},{"en":"They had already left by noon.","ua":"had already left","note":"had + adverb + V3"}],"warning":"Do not say She had went or I had finish."}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["Past Perfect = had + V3.","The same form had works with all subjects.","After had, use V3.","The tense shows that an action happened before another past action or was already complete by a past moment."]}
